title: luzula plumosa subsp. reflexa
intro: Luzula plumosa subsp. reflexa, also known as Reflexed Woodrush, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe, characterized by its narrow leaves and small, white or pale purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. Reflexed Woodrush can be used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems, and can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture.
